paths.notes: This file itself. Only writable by `code-architect owners bootstrap` or `owners migrate`; never a general edit target.
Config dir for CA-managed lockfiles + contracts. Run.lock is handled atomically by safe-json; humans review anything else.

Mesh-API runtime DB plus SQLite sidecars (-wal, -shm, -journal). Kai writes via the mesh-API daemon; CA must never touch any of these files directly — touching a sidecar corrupts the DB just as surely as touching the DB.
Library-test code. Mechanical refactors common; DA-gated.
Shared library code (safe-json, todayET helpers, etc). The CA pattern explicitly targets this lane for cross-fleet helper portability.
Telegram-bot utility code (formatters, etc). Low blast radius; DA-gated.
Evaluation harness. Refactor-friendly; DA-gated.
Claude Code hooks. Behavior-critical; humans review.
Mesh-API source. Contract surface; never bypass-eligible.
Request middleware. Auth/validation layer; humans review.
HTTP route handlers. Contract surface; humans review.
Telegram bot commands/callbacks/features/crons/verify. User-facing behavior; humans review. (Utils carved out above.)
Catch-all for daemons (cfo-agent.js, conductor-agent.js, etc), maintenance scripts, tools. Default to human review until a sub-lane is carved out explicitly.
Top-level test tree (incl. tests/e2e). Mechanical refactors common; DA-gated.
Kai's knowledge base (incl. agent manifests). Kai writes; CA never. CA may read for graph-walk.
Kai's runtime memory. Kai's own; CA never writes.
Kai's activity logs. Kai writes; CA never.
Kai's data dir. Owned by Kai's runtime.
Kai's scratch dir. Owned by Kai's runtime.
PM2 process config — deploy-pipeline territory. CLAUDE.md T3 + DA-mandatory.
Container build. Deploy-pipeline; humans review.
CI/CD workflows. Deploy-pipeline; CLAUDE.md T3 + DA-mandatory.
Lockfile churn from `npm install` is mechanical; DA-gated.
Dependencies + npm scripts. Humans review additions/removals.

fallback.notes: Default-deny posture: anything not matched above (root docs/state .md files, dashboard/, explainers/, templates/, imessage/, skills/, src/, tools/, docs/, etc) requires human review.
